title: Retirement Income Calculator: How Long Will Your Savings
description: Most retirees underestimate this crucial expense—find out exactly when your portfolio might hit zero.

schemas.headline: Retirement Income Calculator: How Long Will Your Savings Last?
schemas.description: Most retirees underestimate this crucial expense—find out exactly when your portfolio might hit zero. Adjust your withdrawal rate and see the surprising results instantly.

mainEntity.name: What is a retirement income calculator?

acceptedAnswer.text: A retirement income calculator is a tool that projects how your savings and investments will perform over time when you start taking regular withdrawals. It accounts for market returns and inflation to estimate when your money might run out.

mainEntity.name: How long will my retirement savings last?

acceptedAnswer.text: How long your retirement savings last depends on your starting portfolio size, your annual withdrawal amount, the inflation rate, and your investment returns. Using a Monte Carlo simulation can help provide a statistical probability of success.

mainEntity.name: How do I use a retirement drawdown calculator?

acceptedAnswer.text: To use a retirement drawdown calculator, input your total savings, the amount you plan to withdraw each year, and your expected rate of return and inflation. The calculator will model the drawdown phase of your retirement.

mainEntity.name: Does the 4% rule work for early retirement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 4% rule was designed for a 30-year traditional retirement. For early retirees planning for 40 to 50 years of withdrawals, a more conservative rate like 3% to 3.5% is often recommended to reduce the risk of outliving your money.

mainEntity.name: What happens if inflation is higher than expected?

acceptedAnswer.text: High inflation erodes your purchasing power, meaning you have to withdraw more money to maintain your standard of living. This increases your withdrawal rate and significantly depletes your portfolio faster.
